rationale: X is marginally better. Both abstracts state a specific diagnostic gap, provide a derived PF cutoff, cohort level sensitivities with confidence intervals, specificity, and AUC. X has stronger HR format fit because it includes an OBJECTIVE section and more clearly states the reference comparison for diagnostic accuracy. Both conclusions slightly overstate the evidence by saying PF improves PVI recognition, since the results mainly validate nearfield versus farfield discrimination and do not show improved procedural or clinical outcomes.
loser_weakness: Y most lacked explicit linkage to a reference standard and had slightly weaker journal format fit because the hypothesis was embedded in BACKGROUND rather than separated as an OBJECTIVE.
